neuro_disease: intellectual disability and cognitive function (cortical thickness GWAS); hearing loss (Nptn mutation)
differentiation_role: Np65 regulates synaptic transmission; chaperones Ca2+ pump ATP2B1 to modulate Ca2+ clearance; expressed in developing brain and pancreas
one_line_summary: Immunoglobulin superfamily adhesion molecule that promotes LTP via p38MAPK/FGFR1, stabilizes the Ca2+-ATPase ATP2B1, and is required for synaptic formation and hearing

molecular_action: Presynaptic active zone scaffold (CAZ); organizes synaptic vesicle clusters at active zone; regulates vesicle docking/fusion machinery; mediates SIAH1-dependent presynaptic ubiquitination; regulates presynaptic autophagy (ATG5); transduces synapse-to-nucleus signals via transcriptional corepressor
key_substrates: Synaptic vesicle clusters; SIAH1; ATG5; RIM; Piccolo; RIM-binding proteins
subcellular: presynaptic active zone; Golgi (Piccolo-Bassoon transport vesicles)
tissues: brain (ubiquitous CNS); inner ear (hair cell synaptic ribbon)
pathways: Presynaptic active zone assembly; vesicle docking/fusion; presynaptic autophagy; ubiquitin-proteasome pathway; synaptic ribbon formation in hair cells
ko_phenotype: premature death; seizures; ataxia; impaired righting; abnormal retina rod cell morphology; abnormal CNS synaptic transmission; myoclonus; decreased cochlear compound action potential; abnormal inner hair cell synapse
neural_phenotype: seizures; ataxia; myoclonus; abnormal CNS synaptic transmission; cochlear inner hair cell synaptic ribbon abnormalities; premature death
cerebellar_evidence: Mutations in BSN cause epilepsy; critical for cerebellar and CNS synaptic active zone organization; expressed abundantly in cerebellum; H3K27me3-marked late gene
neuro_disease: epilepsy (BSN mutations); autism (astrocyte lipid metabolism via BSN iPSC model)
differentiation_role: Bassoon transported to nascent synapses via PTVs during synaptogenesis; essential scaffold for active zone assembly during synapse maturation
one_line_summary: Giant presynaptic scaffold protein that organizes the active zone, regulates vesicle release probability, and controls synaptic autophagy; BSN mutations cause epilepsy and ataxia

molecular_action: Phosphatidylinositol 3,4,5-trisphosphate-dependent Arf GAP and Rho GAP; inactivates ARF1/ARF5; regulates actin cytoskeleton remodeling; promotes macropinocytosis; controls osteoclast podosome condensation into sealing zones; regulates AP-3 complex recruitment to endosomes; modulates EGFR internalization and recycling
key_substrates: ARF1; ARF5; RHOA (indirect); EGFR; AP-3 complex
cell_types: osteoclasts; endothelial cells; macrophages; dendritic cells
pathways: PI3K signaling; ARF GTPase regulation; actin dynamics; endocytosis/macropinocytosis; EGFR trafficking; bone resorption
ko_phenotype: decreased lean body mass; abnormal vocalization; abnormal retina morphology; short tibia; decreased mean corpuscular hemoglobin; decreased pulmonary respiratory rate
neural_phenotype: abnormal vocalization; abnormal retina morphology
cerebellar_evidence: Abnormal vocalization (ultrasonic) in KO mice; abnormal retina morphology; H3K27me3-marked mid-timing gene
cancer_types: type 2 diabetes (GWAS locus); BCR-ABL1 proximal signaling network
differentiation_role: Controls ARF GTPase activity in actin remodeling; regulates osteoclast differentiation and bone resorption via podosome organization
one_line_summary: PIP3-activated dual Arf/Rho GAP that controls actin cytoskeleton dynamics, macropinocytosis, and osteoclast podosome organization for bone resorption
